license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.wdefa3e
title: Detection and quantification of Candida spp. from subgingival areas
author: Sanja Petrovic

steps_raw.instruction: 1. The first day- clinical periodontal examination (full mouth examination at six sites per tooth) in order to diagnose chronic periodontitis and detect the deepest periodontal pocket (with the highest PPD value).

-Record plaque index (Silness-Löe), bleeding on probing (BOP), periodontal pocket depth (PPD) and clinical attachment loss (CAL). Samples should not be obtained the first day, to avoid contamination by blood.
2. The second day- sampling. Isolate sampling tooth with cotton rolls.
-Remove the supragingival plaque by a curette and sterile gauze.
-Place two sterile paper points (#30) into the pocket/sulcus until a mild resistance appeares and keep for 30 seconds. Paper points contaminated by blood should be excluded.
-Collect paper points and place into 1mL of Sabouraud dextrose broth.
- Use sterile curette to obtain samples of the complete subgingival biofilm and inoculated in separate sterile plastic tubes containing 1 mL of Sabouraud dextrose broth.-
3. The second day -microbiological protocols. Vortex samples for 60 seconds.
- Streak 20μl of suspended broth on SDA and ChromAgar using sterile plastic micro pipette.
4. The fourth day. Count the number of Colony Forming Units per sample.
-Multiply the obtained number by 50 in order to obtain CFU/ml.
